moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to comply with concentration limits and reduce broad market exposure.
Sell all flagged holdings (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to exit weakness and downtrends.
Completely exit all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) for capital preservation.
Buy XLF as a top-quality, diversified sector setup to improve portfolio quality.
Establish a significant cash buffer to enhance capital preservation and stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation, with a potential ~0.5-1.5%/month return. It significantly reduces overall risk, leverage, and concentration. Expect lower drawdowns but also more modest growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and rebalance the portfolio.
Sell all flagged holdings (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to remove downtrending positions.
Reduce all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero to lower overall leverage.
Buy AMD and XLF, high-scoring setups, to add quality growth and diversification.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for risk-first growth, targeting ~1.5-2.5%/month. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality growth setups. Expect moderate drawdowns and steady growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and free up capital for high-conviction trades.
Sell all flagged holdings (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to exit weakness and reallocate capital.
Buy AMD and XOM, the highest-scoring setups, to press into strong momentum.
Add GUSH, a leveraged energy ETF, to amplify exposure to a high-scoring sector.
Maintain a minimal cash buffer to maximize deployed capital for growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~3-4%/month growth by leaning into leverage and high-scoring setups. It significantly increases both upside potential and drawdown risk. Be aware of leveraged ETF decay and the potential for rapid losses with wider stops.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY and CRWD to reduce concentration and high-beta exposure.
Sell all flagged holdings (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to exit weakness and downtrends.
Completely exit all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to minimize risk.
Buy XLF, a high-scoring diversified sector, to improve portfolio quality for income.
Establish a significant cash buffer for stability and future income opportunities.
income.read: This income plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions, targeting ~0.5-1%/month. It focuses on low drawdown and reliable income over growth, with minimal leverage and high-beta exposure. Note that distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline.
